findings.evidence: grep hits: line 40 `theorem mem_shiftInvariantSigma_iff ...`; line 46 `theorem measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma_iff ...`; both state `MeasurableSet[shiftInvariantSigma α] s ↔ MeasurableSet s ∧ IsShiftInvariant s`.
findings.fix: Keep one name, preferably the roadmap target `mem_shiftInvariantSigma_iff`, and update uses to it.

findings.evidence: grep hits: line 66 `theorem IsShiftInvariant.measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma ...`; line 72 `theorem shiftInvariant_implies_shiftInvariantMeasurable ...`; both prove `MeasurableSet[shiftInvariantSigma α] s` from `MeasurableSet s` and `IsShiftInvariant s`.
findings.fix: Keep the roadmap-required theorem, or make one a local proof helper instead of exporting both public declarations.
findings.issue: `IsShiftInvariant.measurableSet_shiftInvariantSigma` and `shiftInvariant_implies_shiftInvariantMeasurable` are parallel APIs for the same measurable-set constructor, differing only by namespace and argument order.

findings.evidence: grep hits: line 90 `theorem measurable_shift_shiftInvariantSigma`; line 97 `theorem shiftInvariantSigma_measurable_shift_eq`; both state `@Measurable (ℕ → α) (ℕ → α) (shiftInvariantSigma α) (shiftInvariantSigma α) (shift α)`.
findings.fix: Keep one public theorem, preferably the roadmap target `shiftInvariantSigma_measurable_shift_eq`, and put the proof there directly.
findings.issue: `measurable_shift_shiftInvariantSigma` duplicates `shiftInvariantSigma_measurable_shift_eq` with the identical measurability statement.

summary: The PR mostly reuses Mathlib's invariant-sigma API, but it introduces duplicate TauCeti-facing aliases for the same facts. Remove the parallel names or keep only the roadmap-required entry points.
